title: Artificial intelligence continues to transform business and investment strategies
summary: The rise of AI drives tech companies and asset managers, while some analysts warn of possible overconfidence and see a shift towards traditional industry.

title: Oil prices rise due to Middle East tensions and supply cuts
summary: Crude oil exceeds $95 amid escalating conflict with Iran, Houthi attacks in the Red Sea, and pipeline disruptions, while the IEA warns of risks to energy supply.

title: Wall Street closes mixed in a session marked by rising oil and anticipation of Alphabet and Tesla results
summary: US indices fluctuate between losses and gains; the Dow Jones rises slightly while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 give ground, with crude at highs and focus on tech stocks.

title: European stock markets rise driven by the energy sector and corporate results
summary: The Ibex 35 surpasses 19,600 points and the Dax closes above 25,000, with notable gains for Naturgy, Mapfre, and Repsol, in a positive session for the region.

title: Diplomatic tensions between the United States and China increase over technology and diplomacy
summary: Nvidia's CEO suggests using Chinese AI models, the Senate bans Chinese vehicles, and foreign ministers discuss Xi's visit while Rubio strengthens alliances in Asia.

title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic
summary: The investment reinforces the tech alliance for AI chip development, and Anthropic plans to acquire up to 2 GW of computing capacity.

title: Nvidia receives a warning from TSMC as the market anticipates a rebound
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing warns about Nvidia's prospects, though analysts see a possible recovery towards all-time highs.

title: Executives of several companies sell millions of dollars worth of shares
summary: Executives from Best Buy, CoreWeave, and other sectors reduce their holdings, in moves that reflect personal portfolio adjustments.

title: JPMorgan reports profit growth but its shares don't reflect it
summary: The bank beats earnings estimates in the second quarter, but the market receives the results with caution and the stock lags behind.

title: Private equity falls behind as the IPO boom boosts Wall Street banks
summary: IPOs and mergers benefit traditional investment banks, while private equity funds lose ground.

title: Samsung launches a new foldable phone and plans to invest 180 billion yen in artificial intelligence
summary: The South Korean company raises prices due to rising chip costs and considers an investment in French startup Mistral AI.

title: Gold falls from high levels but remains recommended as a safe-haven asset
summary: The precious metal falls after hitting highs, though analysts consider it a good diversification option in portfolios.

title: Europe advances in cryptocurrency regulation with MiCA and the first stablecoin authorization in Spain
summary: The Bank of Spain authorizes Criptan to operate with regulated stablecoins, within the framework of the new European MiCA regulation that generates debate in the sector.

title: The market eagerly awaits the quarterly results of Alphabet and Tesla
summary: Investors prepare for the earnings of the two major tech companies, which will set the tone for the sector in the second half of the year.
